# Statamarkdown

This is a collection of R functions that extends knitr’s capability for
using Stata as a language engine. They have no use if you do not also
have Stata installed.

You can install this as an R package from CRAN:

``` r

install.packages("Statamarkdown")
```

or from GitHub:

    remotes::install_github("Hemken/Statamarkdown")

You can check your installation with

    library(Statamarkdown)
    example("stata_engine", package="Statamarkdown")

If the package was installed, you should see an example created in a
temporary directory.

Additional documentation can be found at
<https://www.ssc.wisc.edu/~hemken/Stataworkshops/Statamarkdown/stata-and-r-markdown.html>
.

If you would like to contribute to this project, please “fork” it on
GitHub and then clone it back to your computer. Make your changes and
enhancements, push them back to your GitHub repository, then initiate a
“pull” request.

You are also welcome to open issues, or email me directly.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`Statamarkdown-package`](https://hemken.github.io/Statamarkdown/reference/Statamarkdown-package.rd)
  [`Statamarkdown`](https://hemken.github.io/Statamarkdown/reference/Statamarkdown-package.rd)
  : Settings and functions to extend the knitr Stata engine.

- [`find_stata()`](https://hemken.github.io/Statamarkdown/reference/find_stata.md)
  :

  A helper function that seeks to locate your Stata executable.
  Ordinarily this is run automatically when Statamarkdown is loaded.

- [`spinstata()`](https://hemken.github.io/Statamarkdown/reference/spinstata.md)
  : Convert a specially marked up Stata "do" file to Markdown and HTML.

- [`stata_engine()`](https://hemken.github.io/Statamarkdown/reference/stata_engine.rd)
  : Define a Stata engine for knitr
